methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $60M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $29.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Tenna Systems is a Seed-stage Defense & Security company founded by Avner Bendheim and Gabriel Bendheim, currently valued at $60M post-money on $13.5M raised. The peer universe comprises 10 comparable companies across public and private markets: two public comps (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and eight private peers including Scale AI, Anduril Industries, Shield AI, and Saronic.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis applies a typical 2.5x multiplier from the last post-money valuation of $60M, yielding $150M. Peer-median methodology triangulates across four same-sector, adjacent-stage comparables (Scale AI at $29.0B, Stark Defence at $500M, Vatn Systems at $300M, and Ares Industries at $150M), producing a median of $400M, though the peer set exhibits substantial dispersion from $150M floor to $29.0B ceiling.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $128M (low) to $460M (high), with a median of $275M. The 3.6x spread between low and high reflects material uncertainty around appropriate peer multiples and stage progression rates in the Defense & Security frontier-tech sector. The peer-median method's sensitivity to Scale AI's $29B outlier valuation and the early-stage nature of the target create meaningful estimation risk. Stage of next financing, product-market traction metrics, and customer concentration would materially refine this range.
